region_of_origin: Singapore (Old Chang Kee adaptation of Japanese gyoza)

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No specific FoodOn term exists for gyoza on a stick; closest parent 'dumpling food product' (FOODON_00002670) used as category anchor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKM for dumplings used as closest parent; no specific code for gyoza-on-stick exists in the FoodEx2 catalogue.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT concept found for gyoza or dumpling snack on stick.

serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving is a single gyoza piece on a stick, weighing approximately 30 g, as sold by Old Chang Kee in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Gyoza (pan-fried pork dumplings) — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy / Foundation Foods composite; adapted with reference to HPB Singapore nutrient data for similar fried dumpling snacks
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central; Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB Foodo)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for 'Gyoza OnStik' exists in HPB Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central entry for pan-fried pork gyoza / potstickers (NDB 18033 and related entries) and cross-referenced with HPB Singapore data for fried snack dumplings. Per-100g values reflect a pan-fried wheat-wrapper pork-filled gyoza product. Serving size set at 30 g per piece based on typical Old Chang Kee single-piece snack weight.

gi_source.title: Dimsum / Dumpling GI analogous estimate based on wheat-flour filled dumpling data; reference: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for gyoza on a stick. GI of 55 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 international GI tables for wheat-flour filled dumplings and similar dim sum items (wontons, potstickers). Wheat-wrapper dumplings with protein-fat filling consistently show GI values in the 50–60 range, which is classified as low GI. GL per serving = (55 × 7.62 g carbohydrate per 30 g serving) / 100 = 4.2.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~480 mg per 100 g; ~144 mg per piece)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit consumption of Gyoza OnStik due to its significant sodium content from soy sauce and seasoning; one piece contributes approximately 144 mg sodium, and multiple pieces can rapidly approach the recommended daily limit of 2000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (GI 55) with moderate carbohydrate load from wheat wrapper
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes can consume Gyoza OnStik in moderation; the low GI of approximately 55 and low glycemic load per piece (~4.2) are manageable within a controlled carbohydrate meal plan, but portion control is essential as multiple pieces will accumulate carbohydrate and caloric load.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat and sodium from pork filling and soy-based seasoning
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals managing cardiovascular disease should consume Gyoza OnStik sparingly, as the combination of saturated fat (~2.8 g per 100 g) from pork mince and high sodium (~480 mg per 100 g) from soy sauce seasoning may adversely affect blood pressure and lipid profiles if consumed frequently.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Gyoza OnStik is a Singaporean snack innovation sold by Old Chang Kee, a well-known local snack chain, that reimagines the Japanese gyoza (pan-fried pork dumpling) as a convenient single-piece skewered street food. Each piece consists of a thin wheat-flour wrapper encasing a seasoned pork and cabbage filling, pan-fried or deep-fried to achieve a crispy golden exterior with a juicy, savoury interior. The snack is served on a stick for easy on-the-go eating, consistent with Old Chang Kee's signature curry puff and snack-on-stick format. Nutritionally, each 30 g piece provides approximately 65 kcal, 2.6 g protein, 2.8 g fat, and 7.6 g carbohydrate. The sodium content is notable at approximately 144 mg per piece, making portion awareness important for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The low glycemic index (~55) means blood glucose impact is moderate, suitable for people with Type 2 Diabetes when consumed in limited quantities. The snack is not halal-certified due to pork content and contains gluten and soy allergens. It is a culturally hybrid product reflecting Singapore's multicultural food landscape, blending Japanese culinary tradition with local snack culture.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Gyoza OnStik?
Is Gyoza OnStik suitable for diabetics?
What is in Old Chang Kee Gyoza OnStik?
How much sodium in Gyoza OnStik?
What is the GI of gyoza?
Can I eat Gyoza OnStik if I have high blood pressure?

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry for 'Gyoza OnStik' by Old Chang Kee exists in HPB Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Values are adapted from USDA pan-fried pork gyoza/potsticker data and cross-referenced with HPB Singapore fried snack dumpling data. The adaptation is defensible and well-documented but does not constitute an exact source match, hence medium confidence.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central pan-fried pork gyoza entries and HPB Singapore fried dumpling snack data; no exact branded entry for Old Chang Kee Gyoza OnStik was found in approved sources. 2. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA or HPB source data for this food category. 3. Vitamin E is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA or HPB source data for this food category. 4. Selenium is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA or HPB source data for this food category. 5. GI of 55 is an analogous estimate based on wheat-flour filled dumpling data from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 peer-reviewed tables; no direct GI measurement for gyoza on a stick was found. This GI of 55 is classified as low. 6. The food_name in the input JSON was 'Gyoza OnStik' (brand/menu name); canonical English name used is 'Gyoza on a Stick'. 7. The cuisine field in the input JSON was listed as 'Curry Puff' which appears to be a data entry error from the field survey seed record; this has been corrected to 'Japanese-Singaporean Fusion Snack'. 8. Halal status is false as Old Chang Kee Gyoza OnStik contains pork. 9. Multilingual names provided for English, Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, Hindi, and Japanese to satisfy Singaporean dish multilingual requirements and Japanese cuisine origin requirements. 10. FoodOn URI FOODON_00002670 and FoodEx2 code A0CKM are used as closest parent matches; no exact ontology codes exist for this specific product.

Iteration 1: Adapted nutrient values from USDA FoodData Central pan-fried pork gyoza data cross-referenced with HPB Singapore fried dumpling data.
Iteration 1: Documented all -1 sentinel values (vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, selenium_mcg) with explicit notes per G3 requirement.
Iteration 1: Set GI to 55 (medium) as analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 for wheat-flour filled dumplings.
Iteration 1: Verified aliases_by_language and flat aliases list are perfectly consistent — all language-specific strings appear verbatim in flat aliases array.
